generated.b826820790d368b8ae6c97630e53b5238baa7518:
1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal control framework for the document.  
   Expected content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for Automated Quality Validation Rules  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the action, procedure, and expected outcomes.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the procedures for automating data quality validation rules to enhance data governance and ensure compliance with organizational standards.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the domain and contextualize the governance action.  
   Expected content: Definitions, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Quality (DQ)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Relevant to all data management activities within the organization.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 4 - Controlled  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DQ-06 - Automate validation, execution, monitoring, and remediation tracking  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a robust automated quality validation framework.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all data quality dimensions within the organization.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To detail the procedural steps necessary for implementation.  
   Expected content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and execution structure.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To establish automated quality validation rules.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires validation criteria based on DAMA definitions.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Automated quality rule set.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Define validation criteria.  
   - Ensure metadata clarity.  
   - Validate lifecycle alignment.  
   - Document automation logic.  
   - Conduct CMMI automation readiness assessment.  
   - Implement automated quality rules.  
   - Establish governance framework.  
   - Monitor and track remediation.  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To outline the governance framework supporting the action.  
   Expected content: Coverage, themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments involved in data management.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets subject to quality validation.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, automation, compliance.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for data quality decisions.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Establish validation criteria.  
   - Review and approve quality rules.  
   - Monitor rule execution.  
   - Track remediation efforts.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Governance Council and other relevant bodies.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Non-data quality related governance activities.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To define the mechanisms for monitoring and compliance.  
   Expected content: Monitoring, compliance alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Establish KPIs for rule execution and remediation tracking.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Ensure alignment with internal audit function for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Define accountability timelines for addressing non-compliance.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: (Only when Score = 5) Document evidence of continuous improvement and optimization efforts.  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document will be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Scheduled reviews every six months.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office is responsible for document maintenance.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Validation criteria template, automation logic template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK, CMMI guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous audit reports, stakeholder review feedback.
10. Evidence Required
- Approved document with version control
- Formal approval record
- RACI matrix attached
- Repository registration ID
- Stakeholder validation evidence

11. Acceptance Criteria
- Document formally approved
- Scope clearly defined
- Roles validated
- Deliverables stored in official repository
- Communication recorded

12. Control Owner
- Primary Owner: Domain Owner or Data Governance Office
- Oversight Authority: Data Governance Council
- Escalation Path: Chief Data Officer

13. Performance Metrics
- % of domains with approved artifacts
- % of reviews within SLA
- % of procedures with valid evidence
- Average update cycle time
- Non-compliance incidents per cycle

14. Monitoring Dashboard
- Domain compliance status
- Pending review alerts
- Governance heatmap
- KPI performance summary

15. Compliance Validation Cycle
- Mandatory semiannual review
- Annual internal audit
- Cross-domain validation
- RACI revalidation
- Escalation protocol

1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the formal identification and versioning of the document.  
   Expected Content: Title, version number, status, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample Elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Data Governance Artifact for Quality Governance  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author  

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected Content: Summary of the action, procedure, and expected outcomes.  
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the procedure for assigning stewardship roles for quality governance, validation, and remediation, ensuring data quality requirements are effectively consolidated and managed.  

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance action.  
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning.  
   Sample Elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Quality (DQ)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with a focus on data-driven decision-making.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 1 - Initial  

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being taken within the governance framework.  
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample Elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DQ-02 - Assign stewardship roles for quality governance, validation, and remediation  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish clear stewardship roles to enhance data quality governance.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applicable to all data quality requirements across the organization.  

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the step-by-step process for executing the action.  
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.  
   Sample Elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To consolidate data quality requirements effectively.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Completion of DQ-01 is required.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Quality Requirement Register.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Gather Quality Requirements  
   - Validate Requirements  
   - Align with Quality Checkpoints  
   - Confirm Stewardship and Ownership  
   - Prioritize Requirements  
   - Compile Quality Requirement Register  
   - Review and Approve  
   - Establish Stewardship Checkpoints  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To define the organizational and governance structures supporting the action.  
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Data Governance Office oversees the implementation.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets related to quality governance.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, stewardship, accountability.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Governance Council holds primary decision authority.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Identify quality requirements  
   - Validate against metadata principles  
   - Assign stewardship roles  
   - Monitor compliance  
   - Review quality controls  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Management and Data Security governance.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes data quality requirements not aligned with organizational objectives.  

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To establish monitoring, compliance, and escalation mechanisms for the governance action.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring and KPIs, compliance alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample Elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Regular reviews of quality requirements compliance.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Internal Audit Function to verify adherence to governance standards.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Defined pathways for escalating unresolved quality issues to the Data Governance Council.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: (Not applicable for Score 1)  

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and ownership of the document itself.  
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.  
   Sample Elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Office.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office is responsible for document maintenance.  

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.  
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample Elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Quality Requirement Register template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Examples of validated quality requirements.

1. Document Control  
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the document, ensuring traceability and accountability.  
   Expected content: Document title, versioning details, approval authority, and revision history.  
   Sample elements:  
   1.1 Document Title: Data Quality Council Charter  
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft  
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council  
   1.4 Revision History: Initial creation on [Date], revisions on [Date].

2. Executive Summary  
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.  
   Expected content: Summary of the charter's purpose, key objectives, and expected impact on data quality governance.  
   Sample elements: This document outlines the charter for the Data Quality Council, aimed at enhancing data quality governance through defined roles and responsibilities.

3. Domain and Context Framing  
   Purpose: To contextualize the document within the organizational framework and define its domain.  
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning based on the maturity score.  
   Sample elements:  
   3.1 Domain Definition: Data Quality (DQ)  
   3.2 Organizational Context: This charter is applicable across all business units managing data assets.  
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 2 - Governance structures and decision authority are formally documented and approved.

4. Action Definition  
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being addressed by the charter.  
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.  
   Sample elements:  
   4.1 Action Code and Title: DQ-03 - Approve governed quality dimensions, measurement policies, and validation rules  
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a formalized governance structure for data quality dimensions.  
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: This action encompasses all data quality initiatives across the organization.

5. Procedure Definition  
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary for establishing the Data Quality Council charter.  
   Expected content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.  
   Sample elements:  
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To create and approve a charter for the Data Quality Council.  
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires DQ-02 deliverables.  
   5.3 Deliverables: Approved charter document.  
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:  
   - Assemble the DQC Team  
   - Draft the Charter  
   - Validate Governance Alignment  
   - Incorporate DCAM Techniques  
   - Document the Charter  
   - Circulate for Review  
   - Finalize and Approve the Charter  
   - Communicate the Approved Charter  

6. Governance Structural Components  
   Purpose: To define the structural elements that support the governance of data quality.  
   Expected content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, organizational structure, decision authority model, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.  
   Sample elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All business units involved in data management.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All data assets subject to quality governance.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, measurement policies, validation rules.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Quality Council reporting to the Data Governance Council.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles for Data Owners and Data Stewards.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Establish data quality metrics  
   - Review and approve measurement policies  
   - Validate data quality dimensions  
   - Monitor compliance with validation rules  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with the Data Governance Office.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes non-governed data assets.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To outline the mechanisms for monitoring and ensuring compliance with the charter.  
   Expected content: Monitoring and KPIs, compliance and audit alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: Basic monitoring of data quality initiatives.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: Alignment with internal audit function for compliance verification.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Defined pathways for escalating data quality issues to the Data Governance Council.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable (Score 2).

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To establish the governance framework for the document itself.  
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership of the document.  
   Sample elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Owned by the Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.  
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Sample charter template for DQC.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Previous DQC meeting minutes.

   Sample Elements:  
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments utilizing core data assets.  
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: List of identified core data assets.  
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data stewardship, ownership, and lifecycle management.  
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council and Data Governance Office.  
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles of Data Owners and Data Stewards.  
   6.6 Governance Processes:  
   - Conduct workshops for asset identification.  
   - Document quality dimensions.  
   - Define stewardship roles.  
   - Review lifecycle processes.  
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with other governance domains.  
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Non-core data assets.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms  
   Purpose: To establish mechanisms for monitoring and compliance.  
   Expected Content: Monitoring and KPIs, compliance alignment, and escalation model.  
   Sample Elements:  
   7.1 Monitoring and KPIs: No measurable KPIs due to Score 0.  
   7.2 Compliance and Audit Alignment: No formal compliance mechanisms.  
   7.3 Escalation Model: Conceptual escalation pathways for governance issues.  
   7.4 Evidence of Optimization and Institutionalization: Not applicable (Score 0).

8. Document Governance  
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document.  
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.  
   Sample Elements:  
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.  
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.  
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices  
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials and references.  
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.  
   Sample Elements:  
   9.1 Templates: Data quality principles template.  
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.  
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Data asset inventory list.
